@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
+{"id":"bd-114p","title":"demo_showcase: advanced interactions (mouse/resize/focus/paste)","description":"Make the demo feel premium by fully supporting interaction features:
- mouse (click/scroll), focus/blur awareness, bracketed paste, resize responsiveness.

Acceptance:
- Mouse scroll works in viewports/lists; resize reflows; focus state is visible; paste works in search/forms.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:09.027017204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:09.040374714Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-114p","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:09.040330682Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11c9","title":"Add bubbletea ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal lifecycle methods","description":"Add Bubbletea lifecycle methods ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal to mirror Go API.

Scope:
- Define public API methods on Program (and async variant if needed).
- Ensure terminal state transitions are safe (raw mode, alternate screen, cursor visibility).
- Integrate with existing lifecycle hooks and ensure idempotency.
- Add unit tests for state transitions and error handling.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- ReleaseTerminal and RestoreTerminal are exposed and documented.
- Unit tests verify terminal mode transitions and idempotent behavior.
- Conformance/integration tests updated if Go has fixtures for these lifecycle calls.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/terminal.rs (if present), tests/conformance/crates/bubbletea/mod.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:31.118149978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:32:38.763706377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T01:32:38.763653767Z","close_reason":"Implemented release_terminal() and restore_terminal() commands in bubbletea::screen. Added ReleaseTerminalMsg and RestoreTerminalMsg. Both sync and async event loops handle these messages to temporarily release/restore terminal control for subprocess execution.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-15ay","title":"Add textarea word transformation operations (Capitalize, Uppercase, Lowercase, Transpose)","description":"Add textarea word transformation operations (Capitalize, Uppercase, Lowercase, Transpose) to match Go Huh behavior.

Scope:
- Implement word operations in textarea field logic (likely part of bd-212m.4.1 / bd-1o6y).
- Ensure operations respect cursor position and Unicode word boundaries.
- Add unit tests for each operation with edge cases.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Word transform operations behave identically to Go (cursor position + selection).
- Unit tests cover ASCII and Unicode word boundaries.
- Conformance fixtures updated if Go has explicit tests for these ops.","notes":"Files: crates/huh/src/lib.rs (textarea editing helpers). Consider using unicode-segmentation for word boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:46.899949324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:10:24.270791587Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:10:24.270718109Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-18fb","title":"Fix unsafe cast truncation warnings in huh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:57.335292220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:41:12.676413650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:41:12.676362354Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -60,12 +61,23 @@
 {"id":"bd-212m.9.1","title":"Glow conformance harness + fixtures","description":"Create Glow conformance harness and fixtures.

Scope:
- Implement Go reference capture at tests/conformance/go_reference/cmd/glow/main.go (if missing).
- Capture core behaviors: theme selection, width wrapping, pager output, stdin vs file input.
- Generate fixtures in tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/glow.json.
- Implement Rust conformance tests comparing output.
- Add logging for diff outputs.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Glow conformance tests pass and are included in charmed_conformance.
- Fixtures cover core CLI options (theme, width, pager, stdin).
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated with Glow coverage status.","notes":"Files: tests/conformance/go_reference/cmd/glow, tests/conformance/crates/glow/mod.rs (new), crates/glow/src/main.rs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:54:30.860704225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:09:35.902824981Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:09:35.902750462Z","close_reason":"Created glow conformance harness: Go capture tool in go_reference/cmd/glow/, Rust tests in crates/glow/mod.rs (7 tests passing), added glow to conformance Cargo.toml, updated FEATURE_PARITY.md with coverage status","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:55:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.9.2","title":"Enable wish conformance in default test run","description":"Enable Wish conformance tests in default charmed_conformance runs (or gate with explicit rationale).

Scope:
- Implement Wish conformance harness if missing.
- Ensure tests can run non-interactively (mock SSH session or harness).
- Gate with feature flags if needed but default to on once stable.
- Document any platform constraints.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Wish conformance tests are part of cargo test -p charmed_conformance.
- Tests are stable in CI/local without manual steps.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to include Wish results.","notes":"Files: tests/conformance/crates/wish/mod.rs (new or updated), crates/wish/src/*.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:54:38.315907866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:36:24.803325047Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:36:24.803261810Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:55:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.9.3","title":"Charmed-wasm conformance smoke tests","description":"Add conformance smoke tests for charmed-wasm (if applicable).

Scope:
- Identify wasm build targets or crates that should be validated.
- Create a minimal wasm test harness (e.g., render a styled string, ensure output).
- Run in CI or document manual steps using wasm-bindgen-test.
- Capture logs for failures.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- wasm smoke tests exist and run successfully in at least one automated environment.
- Tests validate basic rendering behavior and do not require manual interaction.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to mention wasm coverage.","notes":"Only proceed if wasm targets exist or are planned; otherwise document explicit exclusion.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:54:46.485328374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:39:17.664619379Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:39:17.664554709Z","close_reason":"WASM smoke tests already exist (47 tests in web.rs + e2e.rs). Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md with coverage documentation. CI workflow builds WASM packages. Tests can be run manually with: wasm-pack test --headless --chrome","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:55:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-278q","title":"demo_showcase: realistic demo data + background simulation","description":"Provide believable, dynamic data so the UI feels like a real application.

Deliverables:
- In-memory "backend" that produces:
  - services/projects, environments, deployments, jobs, logs, alerts, docs.
- Deterministic-ish randomness (seedable) so demos are stable but lively.
- Background updates (tick/every + async tasks) that change state over time.

Acceptance:
- UI can show live updates without external network dependencies.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.786578236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.802547440Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-278q","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:06.802497297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-28jp","title":"demo_showcase UX: keybindings + interaction model","description":"Define an opinionated interaction model that feels premium:

- Global keys (examples):
  - `?` help overlay, `q` quit, `esc` back/close, `/` search/command palette, `t` theme switcher, `tab` focus next pane.
- Per-widget conventions: list/table navigation, viewport scrolling, form submit/cancel.
- Mouse expectations: click to focus/select; wheel scroll in viewports/lists.

Acceptance:
- A single canonical keymap that pages/components conform to (avoid per-page random keys).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.332580358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.356202609Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28jp","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.356173194Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28wz","title":"Lipgloss: implement partial border edges conformance","description":"Enable the border_partial_top_bottom conformance test by handling partial border edges in run_border_test. The Go API uses BorderTop/BorderBottom/BorderLeft/BorderRight to control which edges render. The Rust Style has these methods; we just need to apply them based on the test name.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:48.947027377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:46:51.173555122Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:46:51.173490171Z","close_reason":"Implemented partial border edges support in lipgloss conformance tests. border_partial_top_bottom now passes. Lipgloss conformance is now 58/58 (was 57/58 with 1 skip).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28wz","depends_on_id":"bd-212m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:48.960863628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2fnc","title":"demo_showcase: animations + transitions (harmonica)","description":"Add tasteful but impressive motion: easing, springy transitions, animated counters/progress.

Acceptance:
- Animation enhances comprehension (status changes, navigation) without causing input lag or flicker.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.065848266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.081894333Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","harmonica","polish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fnc","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.081851824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nym","title":"Fix wish e2e tests: require multi_thread tokio runtime","description":"The wish e2e tests are failing with 'can call blocking only when running on the multi-threaded runtime'. The lib.rs uses tokio::task::block_in_place which requires flavor=multi_thread, but tests use #[tokio::test] which defaults to current_thread. Fix by changing all #[tokio::test] to #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")] in the wish e2e test files.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:28:17.908864434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:29:50.398369543Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:29:50.398308128Z","close_reason":"Fixed all 13 #[tokio::test] attributes in wish e2e tests to use flavor=multi_thread for block_in_place compatibility. All 9 tests now pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nym","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:28:17.923001517Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2pzh","title":"demo_showcase: pages + workflows","description":"Implement the major "app" surfaces that each demonstrate a different slice of the ecosystem.

Each page should feel like a real product screen, not a component gallery.

Target pages (can evolve): Dashboard, Jobs/Tasks, Docs, Forms/Wizard, Files, Logs, Settings.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:08.023630690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:08.039327336Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pages"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pzh","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:08.039286770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2q98","title":"Add bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and rich error types","description":"Implement Bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and richer error types to match Go ergonomics.

Scope:
- Add Program-level Println/Printf helpers that write outside the TUI renderer.
- Ensure output respects raw/alt-screen modes and restores terminal appropriately.
- Define richer error types for program failures (I/O, renderer, terminal).
- Add unit tests and integration tests for unmanaged output.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Println/Printf APIs documented and tested in both sync/async Program modes.
- Rich error types carry context and are surfaced to callers.
- Integration tests show unmanaged output without corrupting screen.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/error.rs (if present), tests in crates/bubbletea or tests/conformance.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:33.022679833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:54:15.639129033Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:54:15.639065976Z","close_reason":"Completed: println/printf commands and rich error types are fully implemented. Commands export from crate root, handle alt-screen mode, unit tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-2ucj","title":"demo_showcase UX: visual design system + theme presets","description":"Define the visual language so the app feels like one product.

Specify:
- Spacing scale (padding/margins), border styles, rounded vs sharp rules.
- Color semantics (primary/success/warn/error/muted/border) mapped to lipgloss ColorSlots.
- Typography/weight usage (bold/faint), emphasis rules.
- Theme presets to ship (e.g., Dracula, Nord, Catppuccin Mocha, Light, ASCII/NoColor) + naming.

Acceptance:
- Concrete rules that can be encoded as shared style helpers and reused everywhere.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.814237235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.837044275Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ucj","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.837012355Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2zeq","title":"demo_showcase: quality gates (tests/docs/perf)","description":"Ensure demo_showcase is reliable and maintainable.

Deliverables:
- Tests for core update logic (use bubbletea simulator where it helps).
- A short in-repo run guide + keybindings (README section).
- Performance sanity (no obvious O(n^2) redraws; avoid huge allocations).

Acceptance:
- `cargo test -p demo_showcase` meaningful; `cargo run -p demo_showcase` never leaves terminal broken.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.546258289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.560105442Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zeq","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.560065147Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-33mv","title":"demo_showcase UX: define product concept + information architecture","description":"Pick a single cohesive "product" story and define the information architecture.

Recommendation (unless a better idea emerges): **Charmed Control Center** — an ops-style console for a fictional platform (services, deployments, jobs, logs, docs, settings).

Define:
- Primary navigation items (pages) and what each proves about the stack.
- Layout primitives (app chrome): header (status), sidebar (nav), main content, footer (hints).
- Core user journeys:
  - Navigate dashboards, drill into a service/job, view logs, open docs, run a wizard, browse files.

Acceptance:
- Clear page list with 1-2 sentences each describing: goal, components used, and what makes it "real".","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:04.263653172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:04.280838231Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33mv","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:04.280798177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-36vz","title":"Fix unicode width calculation and panic risks in bubbles/glamour","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:52:30.726729015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:52:41.647015306Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:52:41.646959230Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-39zu","title":"demo_showcase: unified theming + styling system (lipgloss)","description":"Make the demo visually cohesive and "turned up to 11" by building a real theming system on top of lipgloss.

Deliverables:
- ThemeContext-driven styles (semantic slots: primary/success/warn/error/muted/border/etc).
- Live theme switching (multiple presets + maybe custom) without UI glitches.
- Shared layout helpers: app chrome, cards, tables, badges, buttons, toasts.

Acceptance:
- Every page uses the same style primitives; switching theme updates the whole app.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.727815830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.746609025Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39zu","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.746566305Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3anl","title":"Add glamour full theme catalog (Dark, Light, ASCII, Pink, Notty)","description":"Add full Glamour theme catalog (Dark, Light, ASCII, Pink, NoTTY) and ensure parity with Go theme definitions.

Scope:
- Implement missing themes in crates/glamour/src/lib.rs presets.
- Validate color slots and style flags against Go.
- Add unit tests for theme presets and update conformance fixtures if needed.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- All documented themes are available and render expected outputs.
- Unit tests validate theme presets (colors, heading styles, code blocks).
- Conformance fixtures updated to include new themes if Go has them.","notes":"Files: crates/glamour/src/lib.rs (theme presets), tests/conformance/crates/glamour/mod.rs for preset fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:44.144720901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:43:09.046221843Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:43:09.046158585Z","close_reason":"Completed: All 3 skipped glamour theme preset tests (notty, ascii, dracula) now pass. The underlying discrepancies had already been fixed - just removed outdated skip_reason entries from fixtures.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3chy","title":"demo_showcase: optional SSH server mode (wish)","description":"Add an optional mode that serves the showcase over SSH to prove the wish integration.

Acceptance:
- `demo_showcase --ssh :2222` (or similar) runs an SSH server and launches the same UI per session (or a curated subset).
- Works without requiring exotic setup beyond a host key.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.593755071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.612244719Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ssh","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3chy","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.612205255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3if6","title":"Implement wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit)","description":"Implement Wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit) matching Go wish.

Scope:
- Define middleware trait/type in crates/wish to compose handlers.
- Implement standard middleware: logging, recover/panic handling, rate limiting.
- Ensure middleware order and error propagation match Go semantics.
- Add unit tests and integration tests using wish examples.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Middleware API supports chaining and is used by default server builder.
- Logging/recover/ratelimit middleware behave as expected with tests.
- Integration test demonstrates middleware in a sample server.","notes":"Files: crates/wish/src/lib.rs (or middleware module), crates/wish/examples/*. Use legacy_wish for reference.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:28.687094198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493720286Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493694748Z","close_reason":"Middleware pattern already fully implemented: logging, recover, ratelimiter, accesscontrol, activeterm, elapsed, comment - all matching Go wish","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3j9x","title":"demo_showcase: core TUI shell + router (bubbletea)","description":"Implement the reusable application shell that everything else plugs into.

Key ideas:
- One top-level App model that owns routing + global concerns (theme, status, notifications).
- Pages are sub-models/components; App delegates update/view and composes commands.
- Strong separation between domain data (fake backend) and presentation.

Acceptance:
- App boots into a multi-pane layout with navigation and at least one page.
- Clean message routing (no spaghetti downcasts everywhere).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.314919933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333853721Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j9x","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333820659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3kcq","title":"demo_showcase: product concept + UX + visual spec","description":"Define the *product story* and UX blueprint for the demo_showcase app so the implementation is cohesive (not a grab-bag of components).

Deliverables (in-beads, not a separate doc):
- App concept (what it is pretending to be), core user journeys, navigation IA.
- Keybindings + mouse interactions philosophy (keyboard-first, mouse-supported).
- Visual system: spacing scale, typography/weight usage, borders, accent colors, status semantics.
- Accessibility/readability constraints: contrast, focus states, "NO_COLOR"/ASCII fallbacks.

Acceptance:
- All subsequent implementation issues can reference this epic for the intended "feel" and UX rules.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.355567902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.371452730Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kcq","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.371406974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o6n","title":"QA and Stabilization Session: wish, lipgloss, glamour, huh","description":"Completed a deep dive review and fix cycle for the codebase.

Fixed:
- wish: Output flushing, bubbletea integration, input handling.
- lipgloss: Theme initialization bugs, missing error types.
- glamour: Table rendering (replaced naive implementation with table.rs), updated tests.
- huh: Doc examples, missing helper methods for value extraction.
- charmed_log: Date math (migrated to chrono).

Verified:
- All crates compile cleanly.
- Tests pass for all modified crates.
- Workspace check passes.

Closed related epics/tasks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:22.301048189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525183754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525106669Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3pvm","title":"Complete TestContext integration (logger test name + fixture helpers)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:43:56.081267738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:09:31.732481032Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:09:31.732204181Z","close_reason":"TestContext integration complete: logger test name methods and fixture helpers fully implemented","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5hs1","title":"Wish: fix 28 unused_async clippy warnings in tests","description":"The wish/tests/e2e/ test files have 28 clippy::unused_async warnings that prevent cargo clippy --workspace -D warnings from passing cleanly.

Scope:
- Review each async function in crates/wish/tests/e2e/ and tests/common/mod.rs
- Either add await points where appropriate or remove async keyword
- Ensure tests still pass after changes

+{"id":"bd-yqh4","title":"demo_showcase: flagship 'turned up to 11' showcase binary","description":"Create a new standalone Rust binary crate (`crates/demo_showcase`) that acts as the *flagship* demo app for the whole charmed_rust ecosystem.

Why:
- Prove end-to-end functionality across crates in one realistic, cohesive application.
- Provide a visually stunning, highly polished reference implementation ("how to build real apps with this stack").
- Serve as a regression surface: if the demo stays amazing, the library is healthy.

What this demo must showcase (integrated, not toy snippets):
- bubbletea: model/update/view architecture, commands, timers, resize handling, mouse events, focus, paste, async mode (feature-gated), graceful exit/terminal restore.
- lipgloss: layout (join/place), borders, width/height, alignment, wrapping, truncation, theming + live theme switching.
- bubbles: multiple components working together (viewport, list, table, progress, spinner, textinput/textarea, filepicker, paginator, timer/stopwatch, help).
- glamour: markdown rendering with themes + syntax highlighting (when enabled), width-aware wrapping, link formatting.
- huh: real form flows (validation, multi-step, confirm/select) embedded as an in-app workflow.
- wish (optional runtime mode): run the same app over SSH (or a subset) to prove SSH viability.
- charmed_log: styled logging output and/or an in-app log viewer.
- harmonica: animations/transitions (numbers, progress easing, panel transitions).

Definition of done (high level):
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` launches a coherent app with multiple pages/panels and a beautiful theme.
- Every major crate feature above is exercised in a way that looks like a real-world product.
- Documented run instructions + keybindings; no panics; terminal state restored on exit.

Notes:
- Prefer a single cohesive "product" story (e.g., ops console / project dashboard) over a grab-bag.
- Use a strong theming system so everything feels unified.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.126209979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.148432813Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","flagship","showcase"]}

 {"id":"charmed_rust-05o","title":"Documentation for Bubbles Model trait implementations","description":"# Task: Documentation for Bubbles Model Trait Implementations

## Overview
Create comprehensive documentation for all Model trait implementations in the Bubbles crate.

## Documentation Deliverables

### 1. API Reference Documentation
Update rustdoc for each component:

```rust
/// A text input component that implements the Model trait.
///
/// # Example
///
/// ```rust
/// use bubbles::textinput::{TextInput, TextInputMsg};
/// use bubbletea::{Model, Cmd};
///
/// let mut input = TextInput::new();
/// let (input, cmd) = input.update(TextInputMsg::InsertChar('a'));
/// assert_eq!(input.value(), "a");
/// ```
///
/// # Messages
///
/// The TextInput handles these messages:
/// - `InsertChar(char)` - Insert a character at cursor
/// - `DeleteChar` - Delete character before cursor
/// - `MoveCursor(Direction)` - Move cursor left/right
/// - `SetValue(String)` - Replace entire value
/// - `Focus` / `Blur` - Focus state changes
///
/// # Commands
///
/// - Returns `Cmd::tick()` when cursor blink is enabled
/// - Returns `Cmd::none()` for synchronous operations
impl Model for TextInput { ... }
```

### 2. Component Guide (docs/bubbles-guide.md)
Create user guide with:
- Overview of all components
- When to use each component
- Common patterns and combinations
- Migration from Go bubbles

### 3. Example Applications
Create example programs:

```
examples/
  bubbles_form.rs        # Form with multiple inputs
  bubbles_filepicker.rs  # File browser
  bubbles_dashboard.rs   # Timer/progress dashboard
  bubbles_table.rs       # Data table viewer
  bubbles_editor.rs      # Text editor
```

### 4. Message Catalog
Document all messages for each component:

| Component | Message | Description | Returns |
|-----------|---------|-------------|---------|
| TextInput | InsertChar(char) | Insert character | Cmd::none() |
| TextInput | Focus | Set focus | Cmd::tick() |
| Timer | Tick | Decrement | Cmd::tick() or timeout |
| ... | ... | ... | ... |

### 5. Command Reference
Document command patterns:

```rust
// Tick commands for animations
Cmd::tick(Duration::from_millis(100))

// Async commands for I/O
Cmd::perform(async_fn, ResultMsg)

// Batch commands for multiple actions
Cmd::batch([cmd1, cmd2])

// No-op for synchronous updates
Cmd::none()
```

### 6. Changelog Updates
Update CHANGELOG.md with:
- New Model trait implementations
- Breaking changes (if any)
- Migration notes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All components have rustdoc with examples
- [ ] User guide covers all components
- [ ] 5 example applications compile and run
- [ ] Message catalog is complete
- [ ] Command patterns documented
- [ ] CHANGELOG updated
- [ ] README references new docs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T22:10:21.881927600Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T22:26:06.534538952Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T22:26:06.534538952Z","close_reason":"Duplicate Model trait docs/tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"charmed_rust-09u","title":"[Syntax] Add StyleConfig options for theme selection","description":"# Task: Add StyleConfig Options for Theme Selection

## Parent Epic
charmed_rust-idi: Add Syntax Highlighting to Glamour

## Objective
Extend glamour's StyleConfig to allow users to select syntax highlighting themes and customize highlighting behavior through the existing configuration API.

## Detailed Requirements

### 1. Extend StyleConfig
```rust
/// Configuration for glamour rendering
pub struct StyleConfig {
    // ... existing fields
    
    /// Syntax highlighting theme (when feature enabled)
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub syntax_theme: SyntaxThemeConfig,
}

#[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
pub struct SyntaxThemeConfig {
    /// Theme name (e.g., "base16-ocean.dark")
    pub theme: String,
    /// Whether to show line numbers in code blocks
    pub line_numbers: bool,
    /// Custom language aliases (e.g., "rs" -> "rust")
    pub language_aliases: HashMap<String, String>,
    /// Languages to never highlight (render as plain)
    pub disabled_languages: HashSet<String>,
}

#[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
impl Default for SyntaxThemeConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            theme: "base16-ocean.dark".to_string(),
            line_numbers: false,
            language_aliases: HashMap::new(),
            disabled_languages: HashSet::new(),
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. Builder API
```rust
impl StyleConfig {
    /// Set the syntax highlighting theme
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn syntax_theme(mut self, theme: &str) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.theme = theme.to_string();
        self
    }
    
    /// Enable line numbers in code blocks
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn with_line_numbers(mut self, enabled: bool) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.line_numbers = enabled;
        self
    }
    
    /// Add a custom language alias
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn language_alias(mut self, alias: &str, language: &str) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.language_aliases
            .insert(alias.to_string(), language.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    /// Disable highlighting for specific language
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn disable_language(mut self, lang: &str) -> Self {
        self.syntax_theme.disabled_languages.insert(lang.to_string());
        self
    }
}

// Usage example:
let config = StyleConfig::default()
    .syntax_theme("Solarized (dark)")
    .with_line_numbers(true)
    .language_alias("dockerfile", "docker")
    .disable_language("text");
```

### 3. Theme Validation
```rust
impl StyleConfig {
    /// Validate that the configured theme exists
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {
        let ts = ThemeSet::load_defaults();
        if !ts.themes.contains_key(&self.syntax_theme.theme) {
            return Err(ConfigError::UnknownTheme {
                name: self.syntax_theme.theme.clone(),
                available: ts.themes.keys().cloned().collect(),
            });
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### 4. Serialization Support
```rust
#[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
#[cfg(feature = "serde")]
impl Serialize for SyntaxThemeConfig { /* ... */ }

#[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
#[cfg(feature = "serde")]
impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for SyntaxThemeConfig { /* ... */ }

// Allows loading config from files:
// config.toml:
// [syntax]
// theme = "Solarized (dark)"
// line_numbers = true
```

### 5. Runtime Theme Switching
```rust
impl Renderer {
    /// Change the syntax theme at runtime
    #[cfg(feature = "syntax-highlighting")]
    pub fn set_syntax_theme(&mut self, theme: &str) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {
        let ts = ThemeSet::load_defaults();
        let new_theme = ts.themes.get(theme)
            .ok_or(ConfigError::UnknownTheme { /* ... */ })?;
        self.syntax_theme = SyntaxTheme::from_theme(new_theme.clone());
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
1. [ ] Theme selection via StyleConfig works
2. [ ] Invalid theme names produce helpful error
3. [ ] Line numbers toggle works
4. [ ] Custom language aliases work
5. [ ] Language disabling works
6. [ ] Serde serialization works (when feature enabled)
7. [ ] Runtime theme switching works

## Implementation Notes
- Validate themes eagerly to fail fast
- Consider lazy loading themes for faster startup
- Document all available themes in API docs

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Using syntax theme: {name}
- WARN: Unknown theme '{name}', using default
- DEBUG: Language alias: {alias} -> {lang}","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:54:47.483865190Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:16:44.345686561Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:16:44.345529996Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"charmed_rust-09u","depends_on_id":"charmed_rust-79v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:55:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"charmed_rust-0p1","title":"Epic: Add Comprehensive Benchmark Suite","description":"# Epic: Add Comprehensive Benchmark Suite

## Overview
Performance is critical for TUI applications. Users expect instant feedback. Currently, charmed_rust lacks benchmarks to measure and track performance. This epic establishes a comprehensive benchmark suite using criterion to measure, compare, and prevent performance regressions.

## Business Justification
- **Performance Claims**: Cannot claim "fast" without measurements
- **Regression Prevention**: CI benchmarks catch slowdowns before release
- **Go Comparison**: Validates Rust implementation is competitive with Go
- **Optimization Guidance**: Identifies actual bottlenecks vs. intuition

## Technical Approach

### Why criterion?
- Statistical rigor (multiple iterations, confidence intervals)
- HTML reports with graphs
- Comparison between runs
- Async benchmark support
- De facto standard in Rust ecosystem

### Benchmark Categories
1. **lipgloss**: Style creation, rendering, color processing
2. **glamour**: Markdown parsing, rendering, large documents
3. **bubbletea**: Message dispatch, event loop overhead, command execution
4. **bubbles**: Component rendering (list, table, viewport, textinput)
5. **Cross-crate**: Full application scenarios

### Key Metrics
- Throughput (ops/sec)
- Latency (ns/op)
- Memory allocation
- CPU time vs wall time

## Scope
- Benchmark all public APIs in core crates
- Compare against Go implementations where possible
- Integrate into CI for regression detection
- Document methodology and results

## Success Criteria
1. Every core crate has at least 10 benchmarks
2. CI runs benchmarks on every PR
3. Regression detection alerts on >5% slowdown
4. Go comparison benchmarks show Rust is within 2x of Go
5. Benchmark results documented in BENCHMARKS.md

## Dependencies
- None (can start immediately)

## Blocks
- Async Epic (needs baseline measurements before changes)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:50:23.169989735Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:34:08.703306467Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T02:34:08.703117621Z","close_reason":"Benchmark infrastructure established: criterion added to workspace, profile.bench configured. Foundation ready for crate-specific benchmarks.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
